cases.title: Plan a change from existing behavior
cases.prompt: Before writing code, trace the existing checkout and give me an implementation plan for adding account-level usage limits.
cases.rationale: The request needs repository evidence and a plan, not a new requirements document or ticket breakdown.

cases.title: Break an approved feature into work items
cases.prompt: The feature requirements are approved. Split the work into small ordered tickets with dependencies, acceptance criteria, and verification.
cases.rationale: The user already has requirements and wants execution units rather than a design investigation.

cases.title: Turn product intent into an engineering specification
cases.prompt: Turn this product brief into a technical specification with behavior, interfaces, failure cases, rollout, and acceptance criteria. Do not implement it.
cases.rationale: The requested artifact is a specification, not code or a project plan.

cases.title: Review a pull request
cases.prompt: Review this pull request for correctness, regressions, missing tests, and scope. Report findings only; do not modify code.
cases.rationale: This is a bounded diff review, and the user explicitly excludes implementation.

cases.title: Review an authentication design for security flaws
cases.prompt: Assess this authentication and authorization design for exploitable vulnerabilities, trust-boundary mistakes, and tenant-isolation failures.
cases.rationale: The requested outcome is an evidence-backed security assessment rather than a general code review or dependency audit.

cases.title: Review a web flow for accessibility barriers
cases.prompt: Audit the checkout flow for keyboard, screen-reader, zoom, contrast, and reduced-motion barriers. Give actionable findings with the affected journey.
cases.rationale: The goal is an accessibility audit rather than implementation or broad change review.

cases.title: Design a test strategy
cases.prompt: Design a risk-based test strategy for migrating billing providers, including the most effective test seams and release checks.
cases.rationale: The request selects verification approaches across a migration; it does not ask to execute a particular test type.

cases.title: Test an API implementation
cases.prompt: Test this staging payments API against its contract, including invalid inputs, authorization, retries, pagination, and side effects.
cases.rationale: The task is to exercise an existing API, not to design an interface or a general strategy.

cases.title: Exercise a local web application
cases.prompt: Open the local application at localhost, reproduce the save failure, inspect browser and network evidence, and report the defect without changing code.
cases.rationale: Browser-based evidence is the task boundary; code changes and a broader debugging workflow are not requested.

cases.title: Investigate a CI failure
cases.prompt: The integration-test job began failing on this branch. Find the first actionable error, determine whether it is flaky or a code defect, and propose a verified fix.
cases.rationale: The user needs diagnosis of an existing pipeline failure, not a new workflow or release process.

cases.title: Create a CI workflow
cases.prompt: Create a GitHub Actions workflow that runs the repository's build, tests, and security checks on pull requests with least-privilege permissions.
cases.rationale: This is workflow authoring, not incident diagnosis or deployment authorization.

cases.title: Set a frontend visual direction
cases.prompt: Define the information hierarchy, interaction behavior, responsive layout, and visual direction for a new analytics dashboard before implementation.
cases.rationale: The request is for design direction before code, not implementation or a reusable system.

cases.title: Build a frontend from an approved design
cases.prompt: Implement this approved checkout-page design in the existing React application, including responsive states, validation, accessibility, and browser verification.
cases.rationale: Visual direction is already approved; the task is production implementation in an existing application.

cases.title: Evaluate a RAG system
cases.prompt: Evaluate our RAG system by separating retrieval quality, context quality, answer grounding, citation support, latency, and cost.
cases.rationale: The request explicitly evaluates retrieval-augmented generation rather than a generic model-output task or research question.

cases.title: Evaluate an agent workflow
cases.prompt: Design a representative dataset and scoring rubric to measure whether our support agent follows policy, uses tools correctly, and regresses after prompt changes.
cases.rationale: The goal is output evaluation, not a retrieval-specific assessment or a new tool interface.

cases.title: Analyze a dataset reproducibly
cases.prompt: Analyze this customer-retention CSV, document cleaning decisions, check the data grain, identify trends and uncertainty, and deliver a reproducible summary.
cases.rationale: The task analyzes supplied tabular data; it does not ask for a database query or a recurring data architecture.

cases.title: Write an analytical SQL query
cases.prompt: Write a read-only PostgreSQL query for monthly active accounts, handling event grain, duplicate events, time zones, and validation totals.
cases.rationale: The requested output is a safe analytical read query, not a data-analysis report or schema migration.

cases.title: Implement behavior with TDD
cases.prompt: Implement the account lockout rule test-first. Show each failing test, make the smallest change to pass it, then refactor while green.
cases.rationale: The request explicitly asks for test-first implementation cycles, not a broad test strategy or diagnosis of an unknown defect.

cases.title: Clarify a business domain
cases.prompt: Before designing APIs or tables, model the insurance-claim domain. Clarify policy, claim, case, lifecycle transitions, invariants, ownership boundaries, and ambiguous terminology.
cases.rationale: The request is for domain language and rules before choosing API, storage, or implementation structures.

cases.title: Coordinate an urgent production incident
cases.prompt: Production checkout is failing for most customers. Coordinate mitigation, diagnosis, recovery, communication, and follow-up while preserving an incident timeline.
cases.rationale: The request is an urgent production response rather than ordinary debugging or a planned release.

cases.title: Prepare an incomplete-work handoff
cases.prompt: Capture the exact state of this unfinished migration so another AI agent can continue tomorrow without reconstructing the investigation.
cases.rationale: The requested artifact transfers live work context rather than documenting a finished system or planning a new change.

cases.title: Instrument a service for production visibility
cases.prompt: Add structured logs, request metrics, distributed traces, an availability SLO, and actionable alerts to this service. Verify that operators can diagnose a failed request end to end.
cases.rationale: The request adds operational telemetry and health signals; it is not a performance investigation or active incident response.

cases.title: Audit dependency risk
cases.prompt: Audit this repository's direct and transitive dependencies for known advisories, abandoned packages, incompatible licenses, stale versions, and supply-chain risk. Do not upgrade anything yet.
cases.rationale: The user requests a dependency inventory and risk assessment without authorizing a migration or broader security review.

cases.title: Audit a collection of agent skills
cases.prompt: Audit this Agent Skills catalog for invalid metadata, broken references, unsafe instructions, overlapping activation, unclear provenance, and maintenance risks. Report findings without editing the catalog.
cases.rationale: The artifact under review is a skill catalog, requiring its structural, routing, provenance, and governance checks.

cases.title: Create reusable frontend foundations
cases.prompt: Create a shared design system for our three web products with tokens, themes, accessible primitives, component APIs, documentation, and a controlled migration path.
cases.rationale: The request establishes reusable cross-product foundations rather than one screen's design or implementation.

cases.title: Implement an MCP server
cases.prompt: Build an MCP server that exposes our ticketing service through narrow tools and resources with validated schemas, least privilege, useful errors, and integration tests.
cases.rationale: The user asks for an MCP implementation, while general tool or API design are supporting concerns rather than the primary workflow.

cases.title: Package an application in containers
cases.prompt: Containerize this existing API with a secure multi-stage Dockerfile, non-root runtime, health check, runtime configuration, and a local Compose setup.
cases.rationale: The requested deliverables are container packaging and runtime configuration, not CI authoring or release coordination.

cases.title: Diagnose an unknown software defect
cases.prompt: Users intermittently lose draft changes. Reproduce the failure, gather evidence, rank hypotheses, isolate the cause, implement the smallest justified fix, and verify the original failure no longer occurs.
cases.rationale: The cause is unknown and requires hypothesis-driven diagnosis rather than planned feature work or browser-only testing.

cases.title: Design tools for an AI agent
cases.prompt: Design the tools an account-support agent needs. Define narrow responsibilities, input and output schemas, permissions, idempotency, errors, and how destructive actions require confirmation. Do not implement a server.
cases.rationale: The goal is an agent-facing tool contract and permission model, not a transport-specific server or general service API.

cases.title: Design an evolvable API
cases.prompt: Design a versioned public API for subscriptions, including resources, operations, pagination, idempotency, errors, compatibility rules, and example contracts. Do not implement it.
cases.rationale: The requested artifact is a general service interface contract, not API execution testing or an AI-agent tool surface.

cases.title: Plan a compatible database migration
cases.prompt: Design a zero-downtime migration that splits the customer name column, including expand-and-contract steps, backfill, indexes, compatibility windows, verification, and rollback.
cases.rationale: The task concerns a database schema migration and compatibility window, not package migration or recurring data movement.

cases.title: Design a recurring data pipeline
cases.prompt: Design a daily pipeline that ingests product events, deduplicates them, enforces contracts, supports idempotent reruns and backfills, records lineage, and publishes quality metrics.
cases.rationale: The user needs a repeatable data-processing architecture rather than one dataset analysis or a storage migration.

cases.title: Measure and improve performance
cases.prompt: The search endpoint's p95 latency doubled. Establish a reproducible baseline, profile CPU, memory, queries, and I/O, identify the dominant bottleneck, optimize it, and compare before and after results.
cases.rationale: The explicit objective is measured performance improvement, not general defect diagnosis or telemetry installation.

cases.title: Upgrade a major dependency
cases.prompt: Upgrade this application from framework version 4 to version 5. Inventory breaking changes, update code and configuration incrementally, verify compatibility, and define rollback steps.
cases.rationale: A specific dependency migration is authorized; the task is beyond inventory-only auditing and has compatibility requirements absent from generic refactoring.

cases.title: Refactor while preserving behavior
cases.prompt: Refactor this parser to remove duplication and clarify its module boundaries while preserving every observable behavior and public interface. Strengthen characterization tests first.
cases.rationale: The requested change is explicitly behavior-preserving structural work rather than new functionality or dependency migration.

cases.title: Coordinate a software release
cases.prompt: Prepare and publish version 3.2 with verified artifacts, migration sequencing, staged rollout, monitoring, rollback criteria, and post-release checks.
cases.rationale: The task coordinates an authorized release lifecycle rather than authoring CI or responding to an unplanned outage.

cases.title: Research with authoritative sources
cases.prompt: Research the current supported approaches for Python package signing. Use authoritative sources, distinguish facts from inference, cite claims, and explain uncertainty. Do not change the repository.
cases.rationale: The user asks for current sourced research rather than repository analysis or a requirements specification.

cases.title: Resolve conflicting Git changes
cases.prompt: Resolve the merge conflicts between these branches by reconstructing both sides' intent, preserving compatible behavior, and running focused integration tests before completing the merge.
cases.rationale: The work centers on reconciling Git conflict intent and validating the combined result, not reviewing or restructuring an ordinary diff.

cases.title: Exercise a CLI contract
cases.prompt: Test this CLI across valid and invalid arguments, stdin and stdout behavior, exit codes, environment variables, filesystem effects, signals, and interactive prompts.
cases.rationale: The task executes a command-line contract and its process semantics rather than an API or a general test-planning exercise.

cases.title: Turn reports into actionable issues
cases.prompt: Triage these incoming bug reports: find duplicates, reproduce credible failures, separate support questions, assign severity and priority, and produce actionable issues with acceptance criteria.
cases.rationale: The request processes a queue of reports into prioritized work rather than deeply debugging one defect or writing general documentation.

cases.title: Document an operational procedure
cases.prompt: Write an operator runbook for rotating signing keys, including prerequisites, exact steps, verification, rollback, troubleshooting, and ownership. Validate every command against the repository.
cases.rationale: The output is durable task-oriented documentation, not a live-work handoff or a specification for new behavior.
